title: broadcast_to_group membership_cache is HashMap<String, bool> but pub_id strings are 64 chars — avoidable cloning
description: AppState::broadcast_to_group builds membership_cache as HashMap<String, bool> keyed by pub_id String (64-char hex). The HashMap is populated inside the loop over channels (which can have multiple entries per pub_id for multi-device users). Each cache miss does a DB roundtrip. The cache key clones the 64-char pub_id string on every insertion. This is an O(num_channels × DB_query_amortized) operation at group fan-out time. For a group with 100 members and 200 connections, this is up to 200 DB roundtrips on the live path (2 per pub_id, amortized to 1 by the cache). For groups at max size, this is potentially 200 sequential DB queries before any message is delivered. A pre-fetch of all membership statuses for the known member list in one DB query before the channel loop would reduce this to O(1) DB calls.
